text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill.
**DrJill:** {Welcome|ようこそ/歓迎} to Let's Learn English with Anna
**DrJill:** The world has many {kinds|種類} of {weather|天気}.
**DrJill:** Some places are {cold|寒い} and others are {hot|暑い}.
**DrJill:** Some have {lots of|たくさんの} rain and others do not.
**DrJill:** Some places have four {seasons|季節}: winter, spring, summer, and fall.
**DrJill:** {Different|異なる} seasons have different weather.
**DrJill:** So now let's {visit|訪問する} Anna.
**DrJill:** She {asked|尋ねた} the question.
**DrJill:** What is the weather today?
**Anna:** Washington, D.
**Anna:** C.
**Anna:** has four seasons.
**Anna:** Summer is {hot|暑い}.
**Anna:** Fall and spring are {cool|涼しい}.
**Anna:** Winter is {cold|寒い}.
**Anna:** Right now it is spring.
**Anna:** And today I am visiting a {beautiful|美しい} place.
**Anna:** First, I need to get {ready|準備する}.
**Anna:** We can help!
**Anna:** We can help, huh?
**Anna:** Hi Bells, of course you can help.
**Anna:** You need a {coat|コート}!
**Anna:** This is a winter coat.
**Anna:** You need a hat.
**Anna:** This is a winter hat.
**Anna:** She needs a summer hat.
**Anna:** Um guys?
**Anna:** She needs an {umbrella|傘}.
**Anna:** She needs a {scarf|マフラー/スカーフ} and {gloves|手袋|/ɡlʌvz/|Wear gloves in winter.}
**Anna:** I like {cobbly|でこぼこの/でこぼこした} hats.
**Anna:** Guys, stop.
**Anna:** Please stop.
**Anna:** Max, what is the {weather|天気} today?
**Max:** The weather is {cool|涼しい/冷たい}, {windy|風の強い}, and sunny.
**Anna:** Thank you.
**Anna:** I need.
**Anna:** My favorite blue coat!
**DrJill:** Now let's hear from some friends.
**DrJill:** They talk about their favorite {weather|天気} and {season|季節}.
**Students:** I hate {rainy|雨の} weather.
**Students:** I can't play on the {playground|遊び場}.
**Students:** Today is sunny.
**Students:** I love sunny weather
**Students:** My favorite season is {summer|夏}.
**DrJill:** Thanks friends.
**DrJill:** Now Anna shows us a popular spring activity in Washington, D.
**DrJill:** C.
**DrJill:** Let's go with her and find out more.
**Anna:** In the spring, many people visit Washington DC.
**Anna:** Yesterday it was rainy, but today it is sunny.
**Anna:** The weather is perfect.
**Anna:** They come to see the {cherry blossoms|桜}.
**Anna:** People are taking pictures with the {pretty|きれいな/美しい} flowers.
**Anna:** In Washington, DC, spring means {cherry blossoms|桜}.
**DrJill:** Now let's join Anna back at her {treehouse|ツリーハウス}.
**DrJill:** She shows us a useful trick.
**DrJill:** She adds a letter to weather words and changes them.
**DrJill:** See the changes the letter Y makes.
**Anna:** The letter Y at the end of a word {changes|変える/変化させる|/tʃeɪndʒ/|Weather changes quickly.} it.
**Anna:** It changes {weather|天気} nouns to weather {adjectives|形容詞}.
**Anna:** A {rainy|雨の} day has rain.
**Anna:** We feel {wind|風} on a {windy|風の強い} day.
**Anna:** A {cloudy|曇った} sky has many {clouds|雲}.
**Anna:** A {foggy|霧の} day has lots of {fog|霧}.
**Anna:** The letter Y also changes a one-sound word into a two-sound word.
**Anna:** Listen sun sun knee rain {rainy|雨の} storm {stormy|嵐の}
**Anna:** {Storm|嵐} is a noun.
**Anna:** {Stormy|嵐の} is an adjective.
**Anna:** It describes weather.
**Anna:** A {storm|嵐} can have snow, wind, {thunder|雷}, {lightning|稲妻}, and rain
**Anna:** For rain, you need an {umbrella|傘}.
**DrJill:** What did the leather Y do?
**DrJill:** It changed a noun into an {adjective|形容詞}.
**DrJill:** {Adjectives|形容詞} describe nouns.
**DrJill:** It also changed a one-sound word into a two-sound word.
**DrJill:** What is your favorite {weather|天気}?
**DrJill:** How do you get ready for the weather?
**DrJill:** See you next time on Let's Learn English with Anna.
